strengths: The signal is frequently triggered, generating a large sample of 25,739 trades, which makes the poor performance results statistically significant and conclusive.
weaknesses: The spec's core hypothesis is completely invalidated, resulting in an aggregate Win Rate of only 9.0% and a highly negative expectancy of -6.25 bps.
The signal performs poorly across all tested sessions and symbols, with expectancy consistently negative, ranging from -4.37 bps on 005930 to -10.15 bps on 000660.
The signal is a strong predictor of the wrong direction; the price moved *against* the signal's prediction in over 90% of cases, suggesting the inverse of the hypothesis might hold true.
The horizon curve shows no improvement at longer horizons, with the Win Rate only reaching 26.0% at 200 ticks, confirming the signal has no predictive power at any tested timescale.
win_bucket_insight: Analysis of the trace file shows that the very rare winning trades (9.0% of cases) are not concentrated in any specific regime. They appear to be random noise rather than a pattern, occurring idiosyncratically across different volatility and spread conditions.
loss_bucket_insight: Losses are systematic and occur in the conditions the signal was designed to capture: when BBO imbalance (high obi_1) is met with opposing deep-book liquidity (negative obi_ex_bbo), the market proceeds to move in the direction of the BBO imbalance over 90% of the time, directly contradicting the spec's reversal hypothesis.
recommended_direction_reasoning: The spec's hypothesis is conclusively invalidated with a 9.0% Win Rate and -6.25 bps expectancy over 25,739 trades. The horizon curve shows WR remains extremely low across all timescales (e.g., 6.0% at 1-tick, 26.0% at 200-ticks). While retiring or inverting the signal are options, the standard protocol for a failed new hypothesis is to first probe a different prediction regime by changing the horizon to see if any timescale reveals a different dynamic before abandoning the concept.
